{"query":"General Electric Venezuela electricity grid contract terms value Delcy Rodriguez 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1720,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/international/article/2026/06/16/venezuela-signs-deal-with-general-electical-to-rebuild-its-power-grid_6754549_4.html","title":"Venezuela signs deal with General Electical to rebuild its power grid","content":"Venezuela now consumes about 16% more electricity than it generates, which is about 12,000 megawatts per day, according to opposition lawmaker Ezio Angelini.
Rodriguez said at the presidential palace that the goal of the new contract with GE is to increase electricity output by 1,000 megawatts per day in the first two years and by 5,000 megawatts in four years."},{"url":"https://venezuelanalysis.com/news/venezuelan-govt-signs-deals-with-general-electric-and-impsa-to-boost-electricity-supply/","title":"Venezuelan Gov’t Signs Deals with General Electric and IMPSA to Boost Electricity Supply - Venezuelanalysis","content":"Rodríguez thanked US Chargé d'Affaires John Barrett for helping establish ties with US corporations.
Mérida, June 16, 2026 (venezuelanalysis.com) – The Venezuelan government has signed two agreements with foreign companies as part of efforts to bolster the country’s National Electric System (SEN).
On Monday, Acting President Delcy Rodríguez penned a memorandum of understanding with GE Vernova, a company formed from the 2024 breakup of US conglomerate General Electric, at Miraflores Palace in Caracas.
According to Rodríguez, the agreement will incorporate 1,000 Megawatts (MW) into Venezuela’s electricity grid in the next 24 months, and 5,000 MW over four years.
The Venezuelan acting leader went on to thank US Chargé d’Affaires John Barrett, who was present at the ceremony, for promoting engagement with major US corporations like GE.
“This agreement launches a broader effort to strengthen Venezuela’s power system through a comprehensive plan that could deliver up to 2,160 MW at Tocoma and 480 MW at Macagua over the next five years,” he wrote."},{"url":"https://www.telesurenglish.net/venezuela-agreement-general-electric/","title":"Venezuela Signs Agreement with General Electric","content":"Venezuelan Acting President Delcy Rodriguez signed a memorandum of understanding today with United States energy giant General Electric Vernova at the Miraflores Palace to modernize and optimize the National Electric System.
John Barret, the United States Chargé d’Affaires in Venezuela, representing the diplomatic interest of the North American nation in facilitating structured corporate engagements.
Ministers Paula Henao and Rolando Alcala also joined the presidential work session, aligning social and technological portfolios with the new electrification goals."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0jyzpv52yyo","title":"Venezuela signs deal with US energy giant to rebuild energy grid","content":"Delcy Rodríguez, who was sworn in shortly after US forces seized Venezuela's leader, Nicolás Maduro, in January, announced the move at a televised event at the presidential palace on Monday.
Rodríguez described the signing of the agreement with General Electric Vernova, the US company's local branch, as "a historic step for Venezuela", which would allow the South American nation to restore what she called "an essential service".
The deal with General Electric was struck under the leadership of Energy Minister Rolando Alcalá, an electrical engineer appointed to the job by Rodríguez three months ago."},{"url":"https://www.hurriyetdailynews.com/venezuela-inks-deal-with-general-electric-to-rebuild-grid-223337","title":"Venezuela inks deal with General Electric to rebuild grid - Latest News","content":"Venezuela has signed an agreement with General Electric for the U.S. utility giant to rebuild its decrepit power grid, the South American country's interim president Delcy Rodriguez said.
Rodriguez, in power since the United States ousted Nicolas Maduro in January, first held talks in April with General Electric.
The discussions came with her government under pressure from President Donald Trump to open oil-rich Venezuela's economy and resources to American companies."}]}

The Rodríguez acting government has sought to address the issue by opening the electricity system to the private sector, with GE and IMPSA the first corporations formally engaged.","score":0.64719695,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0jyzpv52yyo","title":"Venezuela signs deal with US energy giant to rebuild energy grid","content":"# Venezuela signs deal with US energy giant to rebuild power grid. Venezuela's interim president has signed an agreement with US energy giant General Electric to rebuild the country's electricity grid. Delcy Rodríguez, who was sworn in shortly after US forces seized Venezuela's leader, Nicolás Maduro, in January, announced the move at a televised event at the presidential palace on Monday. The agreement is the latest sign that the interim leader - who was a fierce critic of the US before Maduro's ouster - is opening up Venezuela's economy to US investors and companies. Venezuela suffers from frequent power cuts and its power system - which was nationalised in 2007 under Maduro's mentor, Hugo Chávez - is in dire need of repair and investment. But analysts have long warned that a lack of investment in and maintenance of the power grid, coupled with high consumption, have created an energy crisis which has been one of the blocks to Venezuela's economic recovery.","score":0.5441965,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DZq17b7l11A?hl=en","title":"US conglomerate General Electric is to rebuild Venezuela's decrepit power ...","content":"President Delcy Rodriguez saying the project aims to deliver 1GW of power within 24 months and more than 5GW over four years.","score":0.47606868,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/sbsnews/posts/venezuelas-interim-president-delcy-rodr%C3%ADguez-has-signed-an-agreement-with-major-/1463374692500753","title":"Venezuela's interim president Delcy Rodríguez has signed an agreement ...","content":"Venezuela's interim president Delcy Rodríguez has signed an agreement with major US electricity provider GE Vernova.
